title: Settle a DAO treasury fight without an insider rig (real case)

source.case: Gnosis DAO GIP-151 treasury-redemption tension (holders want direct pro-rata access; leadership wants to retain deployment control)
source.note: Encoded from a real, reported governance fight: token holders push for direct pro-rata redemption + accountability while leadership wants to keep control of capital deployment, and critics reject insider control of a contested vote. Positions and the legitimacy red line are from the reported dispute; no party ratified this outcome (real-case run, not a signed engagement). See also ENS/Aave insider-rush cases in docs/demand-evidence-forums.md.

options.label: Leadership blocks the vote with its own dominant stake

presentation.situation: A DAO's token holders want direct pro-rata access to a treasury they feel is underperforming; the leadership wants to keep control of how the capital is deployed. Critics warn that leadership can simply block the vote with its own dominant stake. What outcome is legitimate and everyone can accept?

Token holders.blurb: want direct pro-rata redemption and accountability
redlines: won't accept an outcome forced by insider control of the vote

DAO leadership.blurb: want to retain control of capital deployment

On-chain governance.blurb: the legitimacy of the process itself; won't ratify a vote decided by insider power, not quorum
redlines: no outcome that rests on insider-rigging the vote

partial.plain: Partial redemption plus a deployment mandate

insider.plain: Leadership blocks the vote with its own dominant stake

attrs.label: direct holder access to the treasury

presentation.outcome: The insider move (blocking the vote with a dominant stake) is blocked by the legitimacy red line: it can't be the outcome, full stop. Among the legitimate options everyone can accept, max-min lifts the least-happy party highest and lands on partial redemption plus a deployment mandate: holders get real direct access, leadership keeps a mandate to deploy, and the result is on-chain verifiable. Everyone gets a receipt: no one rigged it.
